physics-concave-mirror-real-image: Draw a concave mirror ray diagram with principal axis, C, F, object beyond C, two principal rays, and a real inverted image between C and F. Keep labels outside ray intersections.
physics-convex-mirror-virtual-image: Draw a convex mirror ray diagram with principal axis, C and F behind the mirror, object in front, diverging reflected rays, dashed extensions, and a small upright virtual image behind the mirror.
physics-prism-dispersion: Draw white light entering a triangular glass prism and splitting into red, green, and violet rays. Keep the prism large and labels outside the rays.
physics-wheatstone-bridge: Draw a Wheatstone bridge circuit with four resistors in a diamond, a galvanometer across the middle, a battery across the outer nodes, and labels R1, R2, R3, R4, G.
physics-free-body-pulled-block: Draw a block on a rough horizontal surface pulled by force F at angle theta. Show N upward, mg downward, friction left, applied force angled up-right, and angle theta clearly.
physics-projectile-motion: Draw projectile motion from ground with parabolic path, initial velocity v0 at angle theta, velocity components, gravity arrow, range R, and maximum height H.
chem-benzene-substitution: Draw chlorobenzene as an aromatic benzene ring with a Cl substituent. It must clearly look aromatic, not like cyclohexane.
chem-newman-projection: Draw a staggered Newman projection for ethane with front carbon dot, rear carbon circle, six H labels spaced cleanly, and the word staggered below.
chem-fischer-projection: Draw a simple Fischer projection for glyceraldehyde with vertical carbon chain CHO-CH(OH)-CH2OH and horizontal H/OH groups, labels readable and not overlapping.
chem-electrolysis-molten-nacl: Draw electrolysis of molten NaCl with two electrodes, battery, Na+ moving to cathode, Cl- moving to anode, and products Na and Cl2 labelled outside the cell.
chem-born-haber-clean: Draw a clean Born-Haber cycle as an energy ladder for NaCl with separated horizontal levels and short labels: Na(s)+1/2Cl2(g), Na(g), Na+(g)+e-, Cl(g), Cl-(g), NaCl(s). No overlapping labels.
chem-vsepr-sf6: Draw SF6 octahedral molecular geometry with S center, six F atoms along up/down/left/right/front/back directions, and label octahedral.
math-matrix-transformation: Draw a coordinate grid showing a triangle and its image under a shear transformation. Label A,B,C and A',B',C' with no overlap.
math-complex-plane-roots: Draw the complex plane with the three cube roots of unity on the unit circle, labels 1, omega, omega^2, axes Re and Im, and 120 degree spacing.
math-integral-area-between-curves: Draw x-y axes, two curves y=x and y=x^2 from 0 to 1, shade the area between them, and label both curves outside the shaded region.
math-vector-cross-product: Draw 3D-looking axes with vectors a and b from the origin and vector a cross b perpendicular to their plane. Use dashed projection lines and keep labels away from tips.
